questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education must an SC associate complete before license issuance?
questions.explanation: SC associates must complete 90 total hours: Unit I (60 hours of fundamentals) before the exam, and Unit II (30 hours of advanced principles) before license issuance.

questions.question_text: A candidate wants to sit for the SC real estate exam. Which course must be completed BEFORE the exam?
distractors: Unit II (30 hours of advanced topics)
Both Unit I and Unit II combined
questions.explanation: Unit I (60 hours) must be completed before sitting for the exam. Unit II (30 hours) must be completed before license issuance but is NOT required before taking the exam.

questions.question_text: An applicant holds a bachelor's degree with a major in real estate. What is this applicant exempt from?
distractors: Both education and the licensing exam
The state portion of the exam only
Continuing education for the first term
questions.explanation: Holders of a law degree or a bachelor's degree (or higher) with a real estate major are exempt from the educational requirements but must still pass the licensing exam.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age requirement to obtain an SC real estate associate license?
questions.explanation: Applicants must be at least 18 years old with a high school diploma or equivalent. They must also pass the PSI exam and complete a criminal background check (state police + FBI).

questions.question_text: How long is pre-license coursework valid for first-time SC applicants?
questions.explanation: Pre-license coursework is valid for 5 years for first-time applicants. If the applicant does not obtain a license within that period, they must retake the courses.

questions.question_text: To qualify for an SC broker license, how many years of active associate licensure are required?
distractors: 2 years of any real estate experience
questions.explanation: SC broker applicants must have 5 years of active SC associate licensure within the past 7 years, plus 60 additional hours of broker education (Broker IIIA + IIIB courses) and a separate broker exam.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes SC's property manager licensing from most other states?
questions.correct_answer: SC requires a separate PM license category
distractors: SC does not license property managers
PM licensing falls under the broker license
Property managers need only a business license
questions.explanation: SC has a separate, distinct license category for property managers — relatively uncommon among states. It requires 30 hours of PM education and a separate PM exam. A PMIC needs an additional 7-hour class.

questions.question_text: How many additional education hours are required for a Property Manager-in-Charge (PMIC) designation beyond the PM license?
questions.explanation: A PMIC must hold a PM license (30 hours education + PM exam) PLUS complete a 7-hour PMIC class. The PMIC supervises affiliated property managers and maintains PM trust accounts.

questions.question_text: An SC broker applicant must complete how many hours of broker-specific education?
questions.explanation: Broker applicants must complete 60 additional hours of broker education through Broker IIIA and Broker IIIB courses, on top of meeting the 5-year active associate experience requirement.

questions.question_text: A person wants to manage rental properties in SC for compensation. What license is required?
distractors: No license if managing fewer than 5 units
questions.explanation: SC requires a separate Property Manager license (30 hours education + PM exam) for managing rental properties for compensation. This is a distinct license category from the associate or broker license.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must an SC licensee complete each renewal period?
questions.explanation: SC requires 10 hours of CE every 2 years (biennial renewal): 4 hours of mandatory core topics plus 6 hours of electives from SCREC-approved providers.

questions.question_text: An SC associate's license renewal deadline falls on which date?
questions.explanation: SC licenses must be renewed biennially by June 30. The licensee is responsible for timely renewal. Late renewals incur a $15/month penalty. CE completion is tracked through the CE Broker system.

questions.question_text: What is the license renewal fee for an SC real estate associate?
questions.explanation: The renewal fee is $45 for associates and $55 for brokers and property managers. Late fees of $15 per month apply past the June 30 deadline.

questions.question_text: Of the 10 hours of SC CE required per renewal, how many must be mandatory core hours?
questions.explanation: SC requires 4 hours of mandatory core topics plus 6 hours of electives for a total of 10 hours per biennial renewal period.

questions.question_text: An SC licensee fails to renew by June 30 and renews 3 months late. What additional cost applies?
distractors: No late fee if within 6 months
questions.explanation: Late fees are $15 per month past the June 30 deadline. At 3 months late, the licensee would owe $45 in late fees ($15 × 3) on top of the regular renewal fee.
